raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A generic review of a research section centrally includes checking whether the benchmark motivation, protocol, results, and recommendation form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The section makes claims about solver performance and benchmark value based on reported protocol and Table 2 results, so claim-evidence alignment is centrally implicated.","R1.6":"Because the delegation asks to review a section, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, concision, and flow are expected responsibilities.","RX.1":"The reviewer must disclose uncertainty, especially where the artifact references external benchmarks, unseen Table 2, and high-precision reference procedures.","RX.2":"The section contains strong claims such as firstness and standardized replacement, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.","RX.3":"The projection must stay within reviewing the section rather than executing edits, experiments, or literature searches.","RX.4":"The reviewer should avoid recommendations that could mislead downstream benchmark adoption or solver evaluation practices.","RX.5":"Any review responsibility should preserve traceability to the provided section and distinguish artifact-internal observations from external assumptions."}}

rationale.R1.2: A generic review of a research section centrally includes checking whether the benchmark motivation, protocol, results, and recommendation form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes claims about solver performance and benchmark value based on reported protocol and Table 2 results, so claim-evidence alignment is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation asks to review a section, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, concision, and flow are expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer must disclose uncertainty, especially where the artifact references external benchmarks, unseen Table 2, and high-precision reference procedures.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains strong claims such as firstness and standardized replacement, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within reviewing the section rather than executing edits, experiments, or literature searches.
rationale.RX.4: The reviewer should avoid recommendations that could mislead downstream benchmark adoption or solver evaluation practices.
rationale.RX.5: Any review responsibility should preserve traceability to the provided section and distinguish artifact-internal observations from external assumptions.
